_note: Source of truth for the marketing strategy. We deploy the FULL $500/mo to ONE strategy at a time — splitting a $500 budget across 5 channels guarantees zero learning per channel. Each strategy below is a complete $500 deployment with its own 3- and 6-month rollout. The owner picks ONE in Telegram /campaigns and that one launches. Organic is a separate always-on track that runs alongside whichever paid strategy is chosen.

constraint.deploymentRule: ONE strategy at a time — full $500 to chosen strategy, not split.

recommendation.rationale: Highest first-order value ($240) × highest repeat cadence (catering corporate accounts) × validated by real customer (review rev_004 — already a repeat office-birthday client). At $500 against a 10× ROAS target the only unit economics that close are B2B catering.

strategies.name: B2B Catering Offensive — Office + Hotel + Coffee Shop
icp: Office managers / EAs at 30–300 person Sugar Land + Energy Corridor offices (~85k matched profiles in Meta)
Boutique hotels & extended-stay properties (welcome amenity, in-room gifting)
Event planners & realtors (closing gifts, milestone events)
Medical / dental offices (patient appreciation, referral gifts)
strategies.thesis: Catering carries $72/box margin and 4–12 reorders/year per acquired account. One acquired office account = $1,440/year revenue. We need 4 wins to clear $5k yr-1.

month1.creativeStrategy: 6 ad sets × 4 creatives = 24 ads in market week 1. Themes: Monday team treat, client meeting, staff appreciation, holiday office party, realtor closing gift, hotel welcome. Each lead-form ad highlights free-first-delivery in 5 mi radius.
month1.killScaleRules: After $50/ad-set: kill if CTR < 1.0% OR CPL > $35. Scale 50% on any ad set with CPL < $20 + SQL rate > 25%.

month2.creativeStrategy: Pause kills from month 1. Top 2 ad sets get 70% of ads budget. Add 2 fresh creatives per surviving set. Retargeting layer launches now (warm pool from month 1 prospecting).

month3.creativeStrategy: Lookalike-1% off acquired catering customer list. Retargeting now hits engagers from m1+m2. Rotate 1 fresh creative per ad set weekly to fight fatigue (frequency > 3.5/wk trigger).

month6.creativeStrategy: Repeat-cycle is the engine now. Month 6 revenue is ~70% from month 1–4 cohorts reordering, ~30% from new acquisition.

strategies.killThresholdsBlended: Pause whole strategy after $250 cumulative spend if blended CTR < 0.8% AND CPL > $40 (mediocre row in sensitivity table). At that point switch to b2c-anchor-flywheel.
strategies.scaleThresholdsBlended: If cumulative ROAS > 4× by end of month 2, recommend owner double-up to $750 in month 3.

strategies.name: B2C Anchor + Retargeting Flywheel — cake "Honey" + slices
strategies.alternativeNote: Pick this if B2B catering proves saturated or if seasonal (Mother's/Valentine's) windows demand B2C focus.
icp: Women 28–55, Sugar Land + W Houston, multicultural family households
Engaged with HappyCake IG / visited menu pages (retargeting pool)
strategies.thesis: Build a warm pool through cold prospecting then convert it through dynamic retargeting. Yr-1 LTV per acquired buyer is ~$110, so we need ~50 new customers + 80% retargeting conversion to hit $5k.

month1.creativeStrategy: 4 creative variants on Advantage+ Shopping: hero product still, cutting-the-cake reel, six-layer slow-mo, customer-story testimonial. Retargeting layer DPA off /api/products feed.

month2.creativeStrategy: Cold creatives rotate. Retargeting now has 4–6k warm profiles. Dynamic carousel shows the SKU each user looked at.

month3.creativeStrategy: 1% lookalike off purchaser list. Retargeting splits between 30-day visitors and 90-day non-buyers.

strategies.killThresholdsBlended: Pause if month 1 cold CAC > $35 OR retargeting ROAS < 2× by end of m1. Pivot to b2b-catering-offensive.
strategies.scaleThresholdsBlended: If month 2 ROAS > 3.5× recommend doubling to $750 m3.

strategies.name: Local Awareness — IG/GBP walk-in surge
strategies.alternativeNote: Lowest-leverage option. Flagged for completeness; not recommended given the rev_003 service-speed risk (a customer waited 25 min for one slice — surging walk-ins makes that worse).
icp: Anyone within 5 mi radius of Sugar Land store
GBP search queries 'bakery near me', 'dessert near me'
strategies.thesis: Drive incremental walk-in slice purchases at $8.50 avg. Math is brutal: 200 incremental orders/mo × $5.78 margin = $1,156 — doesn't clear the $5k bar.

month1.creativeStrategy: Boosted IG posts geo-fenced to 5 mi. GBP weekly posts. Friday/Saturday daypart focus.

strategies.killThresholdsBlended: Pause if foot-traffic uplift < 20% over baseline by end of m1.
strategies.scaleThresholdsBlended: Not applicable — strategy caps out around 3.6× ROAS, do not scale.

strategies.name: Custom Cakes Premium — $95+ celebration cakes
strategies.alternativeNote: Not recommended. 90 min/cake labor × 4/day cap × 58% margin = labor-intensive, low headroom, hard to scale.
icp: Parents planning kids' milestone birthdays (Sugar Land, 25–45)
Photo-cake / fondant request audiences (visual-rich social signals)
strategies.thesis: Premium ticket ($95+) with strong social-share value. But capacity-constrained at 4/day — campaign ceiling is small.

month1.creativeStrategy: Hero reels of past custom cakes. CTA leads to a consultation form (24h notice required).

strategies.killThresholdsBlended: Pause if SQL→booked < 40% by m1 — labor cost makes lower close rates uneconomic.

alwaysOnOrganic.name: Always-on Organic — local SEO + newsletter + GBP content
alwaysOnOrganic.effortRequired: 8–12 hours/week consistent content + on-page work; runs IN PARALLEL with the chosen paid strategy
alwaysOnOrganic.purpose: Compounding asset. Lowers blended CAC every month the paid strategy runs. By month 6, organic typically delivers 20–35% of total customer acquisition for a local food business.

localSEO.deliverable: 8 evergreen pages in months 1–2: catering FAQ, Sugar Land delivery zones, allergen guide, birthday-cake size guide, hotel amenity menu, six-layer honey origin story, gluten-aware options, custom-cake pricing reality. Add LocalBusiness, Bakery, Menu schema (already partially in /llms.txt).
localSEO.expectedOutcome: By m6: ~1,200/mo organic traffic to /menu and /catering. Page-1 ranking on 3–5 long-tail queries (e.g. 'office catering Sugar Land', 'honey cake near me').

newsletter.deliverable: Site capture (3.5% of visits) + counter QR for in-store. Bi-weekly email: monthly bake calendar, allergen-aware tips, behind-the-counter stories. Birthday-club: 'free first slice on your birthday'.
newsletter.expectedOutcome: By m6: ~600 subscribers. 18% subscriber→customer yr-1 = ~108 buyers/yr × $48 avg order = ~$5,200/yr earned revenue at zero ad spend.

gbpContent.deliverable: Weekly GBP post (already exposed via gb_simulate_post). Reply to every review (gb_simulate_reply) within 48h. Monthly photo refresh.
gbpContent.expectedOutcome: By m6: GBP profile views grow ~30%. Direction requests + calls grow proportionally. Improves the ceiling on whichever paid strategy is running.

monthlyTimeline.month1: Set up: 8 evergreen pages drafted, schema validated, newsletter form live, counter QR in store. ~30h total.
monthlyTimeline.month2: Pages indexed; newsletter list at ~80–120; GBP posting cadence stable.
monthlyTimeline.month3: First long-tail rankings appearing. Newsletter list ~250.
monthlyTimeline.month6: Page-1 on 3–5 queries; newsletter ~600; first measurable organic-attributed orders ~25/mo.

industryBenchmarks._source: WordStream, Triple Whale, AdAmigo, OwlClaw, Lebesgue, PeopleLinx, Toast POS — May 2026.
